theme: bonsai gardener pruning between visitor seasons
title: Bonsai Ledger: Pruning Between Visitor Seasons
innovation_hook: The winning policy meters its own amortized benefit online — rotating only when recent-access statistics prove the phase will repay restructuring — i.e., it implements a phase detector, not an access rule.
strategies: never-rotate baseline: leave the display tree exactly as given and only pay hop cost, reproducing the evaluator's own reference (~0.10)

always-splay recipe (the trap): move every requested tag straight to the root before its own search, regardless of season weight or repeat likelihood; wins big during cheap repeat-visit phases but pays the season's inflated per-rotation cost for zero payoff during peak-season one-pass processions, echo-walks, and raffle draws -- on those instances it can land far below even the never-rotate baseline

amortized phase-meter (the insight): maintain a sliding window over the recent access keys, estimate the current repeat-traffic probability from it, and splay a tag only when the estimated future payoff (repeat probability times an amortization horizon, boosted if the tag itself has recently reappeared) exceeds THIS visit's season_weight -- so it splays liberally in cheap, hot phases and gets out of the way in expensive or repeat-free phases

exact online optimum via richer state: additionally track per-branch subtree access frequencies (not just a flat recent-key window) and season-weight lookahead to time restructuring even more precisely, trading a little more bookkeeping for a tighter approximation of the true offline-optimal rotation schedule -- headroom remains above the sliding-window meter
